children.heading: Administrative costs
children.label: 1 Administrative costs
children.text: The term “administrative costs” means expenditures incurred by State boards and local boards, direct recipients (including State grant recipients under part B of subchapter I and recipients of awards under parts C and D of subchapter I), local grant recipients, local fiscal agents or local grant subrecipients, and one-stop operators in the performance of administrative functions and in carrying out activities under subchapter I that are not related to the direct provision of workforce investment services (including services to participants and employers). Such costs include both personnel and nonpersonnel costs and both direct and indirect costs.

children.heading: Adult
children.label: 2 Adult
children.text: Except as otherwise specified in , the term “adult” means an individual who is age 18 or older.section 3172 of this title

children.heading: Adult education; adult education and literacy activities
children.label: 3 Adult education; adult education and literacy activities
children.text: The terms “adult education” and “adult education and literacy activities” have the meanings given the terms in .section 3272 of this title

children.heading: Area career and technical education school
children.label: 4 Area career and technical education school
children.text: The term “area career and technical education school” has the meaning given the term in .section 2302 of title 20

children.label: A
children.text: who is a youth, that the individual has English reading, writing, or computing skills at or below the 8th grade level on a generally accepted standardized test; or

children.label: B
children.text: who is a youth or adult, that the individual is unable to compute or solve problems, or read, write, or speak English, at a level necessary to function on the job, in the individual’s family, or in society.

children.heading: Career and technical education
children.label: 6 Career and technical education
children.text: The term “career and technical education” has the meaning given the term in .section 2302 of title 20

children.label: A
children.text: aligns with the skill needs of industries in the economy of the State or regional economy involved;

children.label: B
children.text: prepares an individual to be successful in any of a full range of secondary or postsecondary education options, including apprenticeships registered under the Act of (commonly known as the “National Apprenticeship Act”; , chapter 663; et seq.) (referred to individually in this Act as an “apprenticeship”, except in );August 16, 193750 Stat. 66429 U.S.C. 50section 3226 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: includes counseling to support an individual in achieving the individual’s education and career goals;

children.label: D
children.text: includes, as appropriate, education offered concurrently with and in the same context as workforce preparation activities and training for a specific occupation or occupational cluster;

children.label: E
children.text: organizes education, training, and other services to meet the particular needs of an individual in a manner that accelerates the educational and career advancement of the individual to the extent practicable;

children.label: F
children.text: enables an individual to attain a secondary school diploma or its recognized equivalent, and at least 1 recognized postsecondary credential; and

children.label: G
children.text: helps an individual enter or advance within a specific occupation or occupational cluster.

children.label: A
children.text: to prepare and coordinate comprehensive employment plans, such as service strategies, for participants to ensure access to necessary workforce investment activities and supportive services, using, where feasible, computer-based technologies; and

children.label: B
children.text: to provide job, education, and career counseling, as appropriate during program participation and after job placement.

children.label: A
children.text: the chief elected executive officer of a unit of general local government in a local area; and

children.label: B
children.text: in a case in which a local area includes more than 1 unit of general local government, the individuals designated under the agreement described in .section 3122(c)(1)(B) of this title

children.heading: Community-based organization
children.label: 10 Community-based organization
children.text: The term “community-based organization” means a private nonprofit organization (which may include a faith-based organization), that is representative of a community or a significant segment of a community and that has demonstrated expertise and effectiveness in the field of workforce development.

children.heading: Competitive integrated employment
children.label: 11 Competitive integrated employment
children.text: The term “competitive integrated employment” has the meaning given the term in section 7 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 (), for individuals with disabilities.29 U.S.C. 705

children.heading: Core program
children.label: 12 Core program
children.text: The term “core programs” means a program authorized under a core program provision.

children.label: A
children.text: subparts 2 and 3 of part B of subchapter I (relating to youth workforce investment activities and adult and dislocated worker employment and training activities);

children.label: B
children.text: subchapter II (relating to adult education and literacy activities);

children.label: C
children.text: sections 1 through 13 of the Wagner-Peyser Act ( et seq.) (relating to employment services); and29 U.S.C. 49

children.label: D
children.text: title I of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 ( et seq.), other than section 112 or part C of that title (, 741) (relating to vocational rehabilitation services).29 U.S.C. 72029 U.S.C. 732

children.label: A
children.text: that is designed to meet the specific requirements of an employer (including a group of employers);

children.label: B
children.text: that is conducted with a commitment by the employer to employ an individual upon successful completion of the training; and

children.label: i
children.text: a significant portion of the cost of training, as determined by the local board involved, taking into account the size of the employer and such other factors as the local board determines to be appropriate, which may include the number of employees participating in training, wage and benefit levels of those employees (at present and anticipated upon completion of the training), relation of the training to the competitiveness of a participant, and other employer-provided training and advancement opportunities; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of customized training (as defined in subparagraphs (A) and (B)) involving an employer located in multiple local areas in the State, a significant portion of the cost of the training, as determined by the Governor of the State, taking into account the size of the employer and such other factors as the Governor determines to be appropriate.

children.label: i
children.text: has been terminated or laid off, or who has received a notice of termination or layoff, from employment;

children.label: I
children.text: is eligible for or has exhausted entitlement to unemployment compensation; or

children.label: II
children.text: has been employed for a duration sufficient to demonstrate, to the appropriate entity at a one-stop center referred to in , attachment to the workforce, but is not eligible for unemployment compensation due to insufficient earnings or having performed services for an employer that were not covered under a State unemployment compensation law; andsection 3151(e) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: is unlikely to return to a previous industry or occupation;

children.label: i
children.text: has been terminated or laid off, or has received a notice of termination or layoff, from employment as a result of any permanent closure of, or any substantial layoff at, a plant, facility, or enterprise;

children.label: ii
children.text: is employed at a facility at which the employer has made a general announcement that such facility will close within 180 days; or

children.label: iii
children.text: for purposes of eligibility to receive services other than training services described in , career services described in , or supportive services, is employed at a facility at which the employer has made a general announcement that such facility will close;section 3174(c)(3) of this titlesection 3174(c)(2)(A)(xii) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: was self-employed (including employment as a farmer, a rancher, or a fisherman) but is unemployed as a result of general economic conditions in the community in which the individual resides or because of natural disasters;

children.label: D
children.text: is a displaced homemaker; or

children.label: i
children.text: is the spouse of a member of the Armed Forces on active duty (as defined in ), and who has experienced a loss of employment as a direct result of relocation to accommodate a permanent change in duty station of such member; orsection 101(d)(1) of title 10

children.label: ii
children.text: is the spouse of a member of the Armed Forces on active duty and who meets the criteria described in paragraph (16)(B).

children.label: i
children.text: has been dependent on the income of another family member but is no longer supported by that income; or

children.label: ii
children.text: is the dependent spouse of a member of the Armed Forces on active duty (as defined in ) and whose family income is significantly reduced because of a deployment (as defined in or pursuant to paragraph (4) of such section), a call or order to active duty pursuant to a provision of law referred to in , a permanent change of station, or the service-connected (as defined in ) death or disability of the member; andsection 101(d)(1) of title 10section 991(b) of title 10section 101(a)(13)(B) of title 10section 101(16) of title 38

children.label: B
children.text: is unemployed or underemployed and is experiencing difficulty in obtaining or upgrading employment.

children.heading: Economic development agency
children.label: 17 Economic development agency
children.text: The term “economic development agency” includes a local planning or zoning commission or board, a community development agency, or another local agency or institution responsible for regulating, promoting, or assisting in local economic development.

children.heading: Eligible youth
children.label: 18 Eligible youth
children.text: Except as provided in parts C and D of subchapter I, the term “eligible youth” means an in-school youth or out-of-school youth.

children.heading: Employment and training activity
children.label: 19 Employment and training activity
children.text: The term “employment and training activity” means an activity described in that is carried out for an adult or dislocated worker.section 3174 of this title

children.heading: English language acquisition program
children.label: 20 English language acquisition program
children.text: The term “English language acquisition program” has the meaning given the term in .section 3272 of this title

children.heading: English language learner
children.label: 21 English language learner
children.text: The term “English language learner” has the meaning given the term in .section 3272 of this title

children.heading: Governor
children.label: 22 Governor
children.text: The term “Governor” means the chief executive of a State or an outlying area.

children.label: i
children.text: an industry sector that has a substantial current or potential impact (including through jobs that lead to economic self-sufficiency and opportunities for advancement) on the State, regional, or local economy, as appropriate, and that contributes to the growth or stability of other supporting businesses, or the growth of other industry sectors; or

children.label: ii
children.text: an occupation that currently has or is projected to have a number of positions (including positions that lead to economic self-sufficiency and opportunities for advancement) in an industry sector so as to have a significant impact on the State, regional, or local economy, as appropriate.

children.heading: Determination
children.label: B Determination
children.text: The determination of whether an industry sector or occupation is in-demand under this paragraph shall be made by the State board or local board, as appropriate, using State and regional business and labor market projections, including the use of labor market information.

children.heading: Individual with a barrier to employment
children.label: 24 Individual with a barrier to employment
A
children.text: Displaced homemakers.

children.label: B
children.text: Low-income individuals.

children.label: C
children.text: Indians, Alaska Natives, and Native Hawaiians, as such terms are defined in .section 3221 of this title

children.label: D
children.text: Individuals with disabilities, including youth who are individuals with disabilities.

children.label: E
children.text: Older individuals.

children.label: F
children.text: Ex-offenders.

children.label: G
children.text: Homeless individuals (as defined in ), or homeless children and youths (as defined in ).section 12473(6) of title 34section 11434a(2) of title 42

children.label: H
children.text: Youth who are in or have aged out of the foster care system.

children.label: I
children.text: Individuals who are English language learners, individuals who have low levels of literacy, and individuals facing substantial cultural barriers.

children.label: J
children.text: Eligible migrant and seasonal farmworkers, as defined in .section 3222(i) of this title

children.label: K
children.text: Individuals within 2 years of exhausting lifetime eligibility under part A of title IV of the Social Security Act ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 601

children.label: L
children.text: Single parents (including single pregnant women).

children.label: M
children.text: Long-term unemployed individuals.

children.label: N
children.text: Such other groups as the Governor involved determines to have barriers to employment.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “individual with a disability” means an individual with a disability as defined in .section 12102 of title 42

children.heading: Individuals with disabilities
children.label: B Individuals with disabilities
children.text: The term “individuals with disabilities” means more than 1 individual with a disability.

children.label: i
children.text: representatives of multiple businesses or other employers in the industry cluster, including small and medium-sized employers when practicable;

children.label: ii
children.text: 1 or more representatives of a recognized State labor organization or central labor council, or another labor representative, as appropriate; and

children.label: iii
children.text: 1 or more representatives of an institution of higher education with, or another provider of, education or training programs that support the industry cluster; and

children.label: i
children.text: State or local government;

children.label: ii
children.text: State or local economic development agencies;

children.label: iii
children.text: State boards or local boards, as appropriate;

children.label: iv
children.text: a State workforce agency or other entity providing employment services;

children.label: v
children.text: other State or local agencies;

children.label: vi
children.text: business or trade associations;

children.label: vii
children.text: economic development organizations;

children.label: viii
children.text: nonprofit organizations, community-based organizations, or intermediaries;

children.label: ix
children.text: philanthropic organizations;

children.label: x
children.text: industry associations; and

children.label: xi
children.text: other organizations, as determined to be necessary by the members comprising the industry or sector partnership.

children.heading: In-school youth
children.label: 27 In-school youth
children.text: The term “in-school youth” means a youth described in .section 3164(a)(1)(C) of this title

children.heading: Institution of higher education
children.label: 28 Institution of higher education
children.text: The term “institution of higher education” has the meaning given the term in , and subparagraphs (A) and (B) of section 1002(a)(1), of title 20.section 1001 of title 20

children.heading: Integrated education and training
children.label: 29 Integrated education and training
children.text: The term “integrated education and training” has the meaning given the term in .section 3272 of this title

children.heading: Labor market area
children.label: 30 Labor market area
children.text: The term “labor market area” means an economically integrated geographic area within which individuals can reside and find employment within a reasonable distance or can readily change employment without changing their place of residence. Such an area shall be identified in accordance with criteria used by the Bureau of Labor Statistics of the Department of Labor in defining such areas or similar criteria established by a Governor.

children.heading: Literacy
children.label: 31 Literacy
children.text: The term “literacy” has the meaning given the term in .section 3272 of this title

children.heading: Local area
children.label: 32 Local area
children.text: The term “local area” means a local workforce investment area designated under , subject to sections 3121(c)(3)(A), 3122(c)(4)(B)(i), and 3249(i) of this title.section 3121 of this title

children.heading: Local board
children.label: 33 Local board
children.text: The term “local board” means a local workforce development board established under , subject to .section 3122 of this titlesection 3122(c)(4)(B)(i) of this title

children.heading: Local educational agency
children.label: 34 Local educational agency
children.text: The term “local educational agency” has the meaning given the term in .section 7801 of title 20

children.heading: Local plan
children.label: 35 Local plan
children.text: The term “local plan” means a plan submitted under , subject to .section 3123 of this titlesection 3121(c)(3)(B) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: receives, or in the past 6 months has received, or is a member of a family that is receiving or in the past 6 months has received, assistance through the supplemental nutrition assistance program established under the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 ( et seq.), the program of block grants to States for temporary assistance for needy families program under part A of title IV of the Social Security Act ( et seq.), or the supplemental security income program established under title XVI of the Social Security Act ( et seq.), or State or local income-based public assistance;7 U.S.C. 201142 U.S.C. 60142 U.S.C. 1381

children.label: I
children.text: the poverty line; or

children.label: II
children.text: 70 percent of the lower living standard income level;

children.label: iii
children.text: is a homeless individual (as defined in ), or a homeless child or youth (as defined under );section 12473(6) of title 34section 11434a(2) of title 42

children.label: iv
children.text: receives or is eligible to receive a free or reduced price lunch under the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act ( et seq.);42 U.S.C. 1751

children.label: v
children.text: is a foster child on behalf of whom State or local government payments are made; or

children.label: vi
children.text: is an individual with a disability whose own income meets the income requirement of clause (ii), but who is a member of a family whose income does not meet this requirement.

children.heading: Lower living standard income level
children.label: B Lower living standard income level
children.text: The term “lower living standard income level” means that income level (adjusted for regional, metropolitan, urban, and rural differences and family size) determined annually by the Secretary of Labor based on the most recent lower living family budget issued by the Secretary.

children.heading: Nontraditional employment
children.label: 37 Nontraditional employment
children.text: The term “nontraditional employment” refers to occupations or fields of work, for which individuals from the gender involved comprise less than 25 percent of the individuals employed in each such occupation or field of work.

children.label: A
children.text: who is or has been subject to any stage of the criminal justice process, and for whom services under this Act may be beneficial; or

children.label: B
children.text: who requires assistance in overcoming artificial barriers to employment resulting from a record of arrest or conviction.

children.heading: Older individual
children.label: 39 Older individual
children.text: The term “older individual” means an individual age 55 or older.

children.heading: One-stop center
children.label: 40 One-stop center
children.text: The term “one-stop center” means a site described in .section 3151(e)(2) of this title

children.heading: One-stop operator
children.label: 41 One-stop operator
children.text: The term “one-stop operator” means 1 or more entities designated or certified under .section 3151(d) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: an entity described in ; andsection 3151(b)(1) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: an entity described in that is participating, with the approval of the local board and chief elected official, in the operation of a one-stop delivery system.section 3151(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: One-stop partner program
children.label: 43 One-stop partner program
children.text: The term “one-stop partner program” means a program or activities described in of a one-stop partner.section 3151(b) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: provides knowledge or skills essential to the full and adequate performance of the job;

children.label: B
children.text: is made available through a program that provides reimbursement to the employer of up to 50 percent of the wage rate of the participant, except as provided in , for the extraordinary costs of providing the training and additional supervision related to the training; andsection 3174(c)(3)(H) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: is limited in duration as appropriate to the occupation for which the participant is being trained, taking into account the content of the training, the prior work experience of the participant, and the service strategy of the participant, as appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: American Samoa, Guam, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, and the United States Virgin Islands; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Republic of Palau, except during any period for which the Secretary of Labor and the Secretary of Education determine that a Compact of Free Association is in effect and contains provisions for training and education assistance prohibiting the assistance provided under this Act.

children.heading: Out-of-school youth
children.label: 46 Out-of-school youth
children.text: The term “out-of-school youth” means a youth described in .section 3164(a)(1)(B) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: contracts, each of which shall specify a fixed amount that will be paid to an eligible service provider (which may include a local or national community-based organization or intermediary, community college, or other training provider, that is eligible under section 3152 or 3153 of this title, as appropriate) based on the achievement of specified levels of performance on the primary indicators of performance described in for target populations as identified by the local board (including individuals with barriers to employment), within a defined timetable, and which may provide for bonus payments to such service provider to expand capacity to provide effective training;section 3141(b)(2)(A) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: a strategy for independently validating the achievement of the performance described in subparagraph (A); and

children.label: C
children.text: a description of how the State or local area will reallocate funds not paid to a provider because the achievement of the performance described in subparagraph (A) did not occur, for further activities related to such a procurement strategy, subject to .section 3249(g)(4) of this title

children.heading: Planning region
children.label: 48 Planning region
children.text: The term “planning region” means a region described in subparagraph (B) or (C) of , subject to .section 3121(a)(2) of this titlesection 3122(c)(4)(B)(i) of this title

children.heading: Poverty line
children.label: 49 Poverty line
children.text: The term “poverty line” means the poverty line (as defined by the Office of Management and Budget, and revised annually in accordance with ) applicable to a family of the size involved.section 9902(2) of title 42

children.heading: Public assistance
children.label: 50 Public assistance
children.text: The term “public assistance” means Federal, State, or local government cash payments for which eligibility is determined by a needs or income test.

children.label: i
children.text: immediately after the State is notified of a current or projected permanent closure or mass layoff; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a disaster, immediately after the State is made aware of mass job dislocation as a result of such disaster;

children.label: B
children.text: the provision of information on and access to available employment and training activities;

children.label: C
children.text: assistance in establishing a labor-management committee, voluntarily agreed to by labor and management, with the ability to devise and implement a strategy for assessing the employment and training needs of dislocated workers and obtaining services to meet such needs;

children.label: D
children.text: the provision of emergency assistance adapted to the particular closure, layoff, or disaster; and

children.label: E
children.text: the provision of assistance to the local community in developing a coordinated response and in obtaining access to State economic development assistance.

children.heading: Recognized postsecondary credential
children.label: 52 Recognized postsecondary credential
children.text: The term “recognized postsecondary credential” means a credential consisting of an industry-recognized certificate or certification, a certificate of completion of an apprenticeship, a license recognized by the State involved or Federal Government, or an associate or baccalaureate degree.

children.heading: Region
children.label: 53 Region
children.text: The term “region”, used without further description, means a region identified under , subject to and except as provided in .section 3121(a) of this titlesection 3122(c)(4)(B)(i) of this titlesection 3121(b)(1)(B)(ii) of this title

children.heading: School dropout
children.label: 54 School dropout
children.text: The term “school dropout” means an individual who is no longer attending any school and who has not received a secondary school diploma or its recognized equivalent.

children.heading: Secondary school
children.label: 55 Secondary school
children.text: The term “secondary school” has the meaning given the term in .section 7801 of title 20

children.heading: State
children.label: 56 State
children.text: The term “State” means each of the several States of the United States, the District of Columbia, and the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico.

children.heading: State board
children.label: 57 State board
children.text: The term “State board” means a State workforce development board established under .section 3111 of this title

children.heading: State plan
children.label: 58 State plan
children.text: The term “State plan”, used without further description, means a unified State plan under or a combined State plan under .section 3112 of this titlesection 3113 of this title

children.heading: Supportive services
children.label: 59 Supportive services
children.text: The term “supportive services” means services such as transportation, child care, dependent care, housing, and needs-related payments, that are necessary to enable an individual to participate in activities authorized under this Act.

children.heading: Training services
children.label: 60 Training services
children.text: The term “training services” means services described in .section 3174(c)(3) of this title

children.heading: Unemployed individual
children.label: 61 Unemployed individual
children.text: The term “unemployed individual” means an individual who is without a job and who wants and is available for work. The determination of whether an individual is without a job, for purposes of this paragraph, shall be made in accordance with the criteria used by the Bureau of Labor Statistics of the Department of Labor in defining individuals as unemployed.

children.heading: Unit of general local government
children.label: 62 Unit of general local government
children.text: The term “unit of general local government” means any general purpose political subdivision of a State that has the power to levy taxes and spend funds, as well as general corporate and police powers.

children.heading: Veteran
children.label: A Veteran
children.text: The term “veteran” has the meaning given the term in .section 101 of title 38

children.heading: Recently separated veteran
children.label: B Recently separated veteran
children.text: The term “recently separated veteran” means any veteran who applies for participation under this Act within 48 months after the discharge or release from active military, naval, or air service.

children.heading: Vocational rehabilitation program
children.label: 64 Vocational rehabilitation program
children.text: The term “vocational rehabilitation program” means a program authorized under a provision covered under paragraph (13)(D).

children.heading: Workforce development activity
children.label: 65 Workforce development activity
children.text: The term “workforce development activity” means an activity carried out through a workforce development program.

children.heading: Workforce development program
children.label: 66 Workforce development program
children.text: The term “workforce development program” means a program made available through a workforce development system.

children.heading: Workforce development system
children.label: 67 Workforce development system
children.text: The term “workforce development system” means a system that makes available the core programs, the other one-stop partner programs, and any other programs providing employment and training services as identified by a State board or local board.

children.heading: Workforce investment activity
children.label: 68 Workforce investment activity
children.text: The term “workforce investment activity” means an employment and training activity, and a youth workforce investment activity.

children.heading: Workforce preparation activities
children.label: 69 Workforce preparation activities
children.text: The term “workforce preparation activities” has the meaning given the term in .section 3272 of this title

children.heading: Workplace learning advisor
children.label: 70 Workplace learning advisor
children.text: The term “workplace learning advisor” means an individual employed by an organization who has the knowledge and skills necessary to advise other employees of that organization about the education, skill development, job training, career counseling services, and credentials, including services provided through the workforce development system, required to progress toward career goals of such employees in order to meet employer requirements related to job openings and career advancements that support economic self-sufficiency.

children.heading: Youth workforce investment activity
children.label: 71 Youth workforce investment activity
children.text: The term “youth workforce investment activity” means an activity described in that is carried out for eligible youth (or as described in ).section 3164 of this titlesection 3164(a)(3)(A) of this title
